{{Citation}}

'''Celebration V''', the seventh [[Celebration]] of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' saga (the fifth in the United States), was held in [[Wikipedia:Orlando, Florida|Orlando, Florida]], [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]], from [[August 12]] to [[August 15]], [[2010]].<ref name="Announce">{{SWArchive|url=fans/events/c5announcement/index.html|text=Lucasfilm and Reed Exhibitions are Proud to Announce ''Star Wars'' Celebration V}}</ref>

==History of announcements==
[[File:CelebrationV.svg|thumb|left|180px|An alternate Celebration V logo]]
The first official mentions of the possibility of Celebration V came from [[Gen Con Game Fair|Gen Con's]] post–[[Celebration IV]] online survey:
{{Quote|We want to make future Celebrations bigger, brighter and more enjoyable.|Gen Con ''Star Wars'' Celebration IV survey|<ref name="CIV">[[Celebration IV]]</ref>}}
{{Quote|20) If Celebration V is held in the spring of 2009, but is not held within driving distance of your city, how likely is it that you would attend?|Gen Con ''Star Wars'' Celebration IV survey|<ref name="CIV" />}}
Celebration V was rumored to be held in [[2010]], according to Steve Sansweet in an interview at DragonCon. Sansweet also had an interview with the ''Baltimore Business Journal'' in July 2008, in which he listed the possible cities to host the convention: Baltimore, Indianapolis, Chicago, Minneapolis, or Orlando.<ref name="TFN">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/latestnews/story/C5_News_And_Rumors_115885.asp|text=C5: News And Rumors|date=2008-07-09|archivedate=20160414155945}}</ref>

In July 2009, [[Hasbro Inc.|Hasbro]] announced a diorama contest in which the winner would have their work displayed at a Celebration in 2010. The Celebration was identified solely as "the 2010 ''Star Wars'' Celebration," though, not as Celebration V.<ref name="Hasbro">{{Hasbro|subdomain=www|domain=promotions.com|url=starwars/page.do?page=splash.html|text=Bring ''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' to Life!}}</ref>

At [[Wikipedia:San Diego Comic-Con International|Comic-Con]] that same year, Steve Sansweet again mentioned Celebration V during the Star Wars Spectacular panel event. He said that it would likely be in summer 2010, possibly May 21–23.<ref name="G4TV">{{WebCite|url=http://g4tv.com/comiccon2009/pressconference/lucasfilm-star-wars-spectacular/40|text=Star Wars Spectacular|nobackup=1}}</ref>

During October 2009, Spirit Halloween stores advertised a contest for Star Wars Celebration V, with a logo featuring [[Boba Fett]]. The winner of the Feel the Force contest would win an all-expenses-paid trip to Celebration V for them and three friends, as well as a free Ultimate Darth Vader costume.<ref name="Logo">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/celebration/story/Celebration_V_Logo_RevealedSort_Of_126438.asp|text=Celebration V Logo Revealed…Sort Of|date=2009-09-22|archivedate=20160215141910}}</ref>

==Official==
Star Wars Celebration V was officially announced on [[December 3]], [[2009]]. Tickets went on sale December 10, 2009. The event was held at the Orange County Convention Center from August 12 to 15 in Orlando, Florida. Many autograph sessions were also held. Celebration V was "star studded" as all others were, with notories including [[George Lucas]], [[Mark Hamill]], and [[Carrie Fisher]].

*International Collecting Panel
*Almost...From A Galaxy Far, Far Away
*30 Years of Empire Strikes Back Collecting

===The Main Event===
*The Major Event of the Convention Weekend.
*Hosted by [[Wikipedia:Jon Stewart|Jon Stewart]], former host of [[Wikipedia:The Daily Show|''The Daily Show'']].
*The Main Event was George Lucas being interviewed by Jon Stewart.
*Fans were allowed to submit questions for the show.
*Jon Stewart was made his own custom action figure with an extra head with a goatee.
*Lucas discussed The Clone Wars, Star Wars in 3D, Star Wars Blu Ray, the new Star Wars animated show, and movie making, and officially named [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] planet [[Stewjon/Legends|Stewjon]], after Jon Stewart.
*Mark Hamill appeared to discuss his new deleted scene on the Blu-ray and to talk about ''The Empire Strikes Back''
*Carrie Fisher appeared to talk about ''The Empire Strikes Back''
*Hundreds of fans waited overnight to get into the Main Event

===Schedule===
*Thursday 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM
*Friday 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM
*Saturday 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM (8:00 PM &ndash 1:00 AM: [[Last Tour to Endor]])
*Sunday 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM

====Note====
Several events, including movie screenings and a ''Star Wars Insider'' presentation, took place after closing.
